title: Full KnowMe hybrid application
scenario: Build the KnowMe reference application with Flutter mobile, Tauri desktop, React/Axum web, and one shared Rust core that owns networking, persistence, inference, sync, and agent behavior.
termination: Stop only when Flutter mobile, Tauri desktop, and Axum/React web execute the same persisted conversation workflow, local chat works without a key, the app survives restart, a clean checkout reproduces the build, and an independent critic accepts the evidence.

prompts.prerequisites: Verify Flutter beta, Rust 1.96+, Node 24+, Tauri 2.10+, a local model path or download strategy, and the Prometheus Entity Management 3.x package source before planning.
prompts.discovery: Read AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, references/arch-standard.md, React/Tauri, Flutter, Rust, deployment, UI, and local skill docs. Inventory existing app surfaces, generated templates, and current KBD/OpenSpec state.
prompts.feynman: Explain how one shared Rust core serves Flutter mobile, Tauri desktop, and Axum web while React/Flutter remain thin UI layers. Grade the explanation and list gaps before design decisions.
prompts.kbd_assess: Assess user-visible outcomes: chat bubbles, ContentBlock/AG-UI events, multiple conversations, local-first persistence, BYOK-ready configuration, launch proof, and cross-surface parity.
prompts.kbd_analyze: Analyze current source, templates, KnowMe specs, mood board, PEM/Zustand/PGlite integration, pglite-oxide desktop persistence, Flutter providers, and Axum hosting requirements.
prompts.kbd_spec: Write OpenSpec deltas requiring shared Rust ownership, React componentâ†’hookâ†’store layering, Flutter screenâ†’providerâ†’domain/data layering, no TanStack Query, Flat 2.0 UI, and public-boundary proof.
prompts.kbd_plan: Plan surface-owned tasks: Rust core contracts first, React/Tauri UI and persistence, Flutter providers/FFI, Axum host, deployment/docs updates, then integrated verification.
prompts.research: Use official/local sources to confirm current package APIs, Tauri, Flutter, Axum, PGlite, pglite-oxide, Assistant UI, shadcn-ui, ContentBlock/AG-UI, and model runtime constraints.
prompts.implementation: Implement one bounded waypoint at a time. Keep networking, inference, sync, persistence, and agent logic in Rust. React and Flutter components call hooks/providers only. Use PEM 3.x with Zustand/PGlite for web state and pglite-oxide/shared Rust for desktop where configured.
prompts.public_boundary_verification: Launch Tauri, serve the production web bundle or Axum host, launch a simulator/device, create and reopen multiple conversations, stream a local-model chat, display ContentBlocks, prove persistence after restart, and run clean-checkout build gates.
prompts.independent_critic: As critic, compare the app against the original hybrid goal, mood board, UI standard, Rust ownership invariant, launch evidence, local-model behavior, persistence proof, and clean-checkout reproduction.
prompts.reflection_retention: Record decisions, failures, launch evidence, missing surfaces, reusable scaffolding fixes, and private context boundaries in Karpathy memory.
prompts.recovery: If any surface fails twice, stop and re-enter KBD analyze for that surface. If a repeated process gap appears, create/update a skill before continuing.
prompts.stop_conditions: Stop for missing toolchain authority, unsupported package claims, repeated launch failure, private content leakage, or inability to prove one shared persisted workflow across surfaces.
